title: Income Approach: An investor is considering a project with an initial investment...
summary: Calculate NPV of a real estate project by discounting cash flows minus initial investment.
prompt: An investor is considering a project with an initial investment of $1 million that will generate cash flows of $200,000 annually for 7 years. If the discount rate is 10%, what is the NPV of the project?
solution: Step 1: Calculate NPV using the formula: NPV = ∑ (Cash Flow / (1 + r)^t) - Initial Investment. Step 2: NPV = (200,000/(1+0.10)^1) + (200,000/(1+0.10)^2) + ... + (200,000/(1+0.10)^7) - $1,000,000. Step 3: Calculate each cash flow's present value and sum them to find NPV = $1,132,000 - $1,000,000 = $132,000.
